one_line_summary: Human ortholog C1orf122 is overexpressed in HCC and promotes tumor cell proliferation; androgen-regulated in prostate cancer cells.
Human ortholog FAM216A (also CCDC166-associated) is overexpressed in HCC and required for male fertility in mice.
Human ortholog C6orf203/MTRES1 is a mitoribosome-associated RNA-binding protein involved in mitochondrial translation quality control under stress conditions.

ko_phenotype: fatal autoinflammatory syndrome with leukoencephalopathy, seizures, neurodegeneration, microcephaly (LOF in humans)
neuro_disease: COXPD53 (combined oxidative phosphorylation deficiency type 53), autoinflammatory encephalopathy
one_line_summary: Human ortholog C2orf69 is an esterase-homolog required for mitochondrial function; biallelic LOF causes fatal autoinflammatory leukoencephalopathy with seizures and neurodegeneration.

molecular_action: transcriptional coregulation, histone acetyltransferase complex scaffolding
ko_phenotype: Koolen-de Vries syndrome features in heterozygous KANSL1 loss; DDH in mice with Kansl1 mutation
neural_phenotype: intellectual disability, autism (in Koolen-de Vries syndrome associated with KANSL1)
cancer_role: fusion partner (KAT6B::KANSL1 in uterine mesenchymal tumors)
neuro_disease: Koolen-de Vries syndrome, progressive supranuclear palsy (17q21.31 locus)
one_line_summary: Human ortholog KANSL1 (17q21.31) is a chromatin-associated scaffold subunit of the NuA4/KAT8 acetyltransferase complex required for histone acetylation; haploinsufficiency causes intellectual disability.
RIKEN gene with no characterized function; human ortholog TMEM256 is a reference gene used for expression normalization and a candidate urinary biomarker in prostate cancer proteomics.
Human ortholog OXLD1/C17orf90 is a mitochondrial oxidoreductase-like protein whose levels decrease under hypoxia in cardiomyocytes; expression is prognostic in stage II colon cancer.

cancer_role: fusion oncogene partner (C19orf47-AKT2 chimeric RNA promotes glioma)
one_line_summary: Human ortholog C19orf47 is a fusion partner in a chimeric C19orf47-AKT2 RNA that activates AKT signaling and is prognostically relevant in glioma.
No abstracts available; RIKEN gene with no characterized function and no available literature evidence.

molecular_action: kinesin inhibition (binding and blocking motor heads)
key_substrates: kinesin superfamily motors (KIF2A, KIF18B and 6 others)
pathways: axon transport, microtubule dynamics, mitochondrial biogenesis
ko_phenotype: Goldberg-Shprintzen syndrome (GOSHS): microcephaly, intellectual disability, Hirschsprung disease, peripheral neuropathy (LOF)
neural_phenotype: microcephaly, intellectual disability, peripheral neuropathy, progressive ataxia
one_line_summary: Human ortholog KIFBP/KIF1BP inhibits 8 kinesin motors by binding their catalytic heads; loss of function causes Goldberg-Shprintzen syndrome with microcephaly and neurodegeneration.

cancer_role: oncogenic (activates STAT3; promotes sorafenib resistance in HCC)
one_line_summary: Human ortholog C21orf58 is an adaptor protein that activates STAT3 signaling by forming a ternary complex; overexpressed and oncogenic in hepatocellular carcinoma.

pathways: cardiolipin biosynthesis, mitochondrial lipid metabolism, oxidative phosphorylation
one_line_summary: Human ortholog ABHD18 is a mitochondrial alpha/beta-hydrolase that cleaves nascent cardiolipin to generate monolysocardiolipin as a key step in cardiolipin remodeling.

cancer_role: tumor suppressor (OSER1-AS1: downregulated in lung/HCC; promotes gefitinib resistance when upregulated)
one_line_summary: Mouse Riken gene corresponding to the human lncRNA locus OSER1-AS1; OSER1-AS1 acts as a tumor suppressor in lung cancer and modulates EGFR-targeted therapy resistance.

pathways: immune regulation, tumor microenvironment, STAT3 signaling
cancer_types: lung adenocarcinoma, hepatocellular carcinoma, endometrial carcinoma, cholangiocarcinoma
cancer_role: oncogenic (overexpressed, promotes proliferation; immunosuppressive in tumor microenvironment)
one_line_summary: Human ortholog C5orf34 is an evolutionarily conserved protein of unknown function that is overexpressed across multiple cancer types and associated with poor prognosis and immune evasion.

molecular_action: heterochromatin maintenance, HP1alpha anchoring at centromeres during mitosis
pathways: heterochromatin organization, centromere integrity, D4Z4 repeat silencing
one_line_summary: Human ortholog LRIF1 is a PXVXL-motif heterochromatin protein that anchors HP1alpha at centromeres during mitosis and cooperates with SMCHD1 to silence the D4Z4 repeat; germline LOF causes FSHD2.

molecular_action: transcriptional repression/activation; ubiquitin-mediated proteolysis; SUMO-regulated transcriptional switching
key_substrates: WNT target genes; EGFR-stimulated transcriptional coregulators
pathways: WNT signaling, EGFR/SUMO signaling, ubiquitin pathway
ko_phenotype: NEDAMSS (neurodevelopmental disorder with regression, abnormal movements, loss of speech, seizures) from heterozygous LOF
neural_phenotype: developmental regression, seizures, dystonia, loss of speech, progressive neurodegeneration
cancer_role: oncogenic (IRF2BPL promotes malignant phenotypes in ESCC as transcriptional activator)
one_line_summary: Human ortholog IRF2BPL is a zinc-finger transcriptional regulator that suppresses WNT signaling in neurons; heterozygous loss-of-function causes NEDAMSS, a severe neurodevelopmental regression syndrome.

molecular_action: microtubule stabilization at kinetochores; CLASP-interacting mitotic regulator
cancer_types: ovarian cancer, colorectal cancer, breast cancer, hepatocellular carcinoma
cancer_role: oncogenic context (SOGA1 upregulated; METTL16-driven chromosome instability pathway)
one_line_summary: Human ortholog SOGA1 interacts with CLASP1/2 at kinetochores during mitosis and plays a role in chromosome segregation and microtubule dynamics.

cancer_role: tumor suppressor context (TOR4A, the human ortholog, mediates DNER-dependent growth inhibition in glioma)
neuro_disease: Niemann-Pick type C (NPC) disease proteome alteration
one_line_summary: Human ortholog TOR4A (Torsin family 4 member A) is a transmembrane ER protein whose expression is regulated by DNER-Notch signaling in glioma and is altered in Niemann-Pick type C disease fibroblasts.

pathways: mitochondrial antioxidant signaling, S-palmitoylation cycle, drug metabolism
ko_phenotype: male infertility; abnormal spermatogenesis in Abhd10-null mice
one_line_summary: ABHD10 is a mitochondrial matrix serine hydrolase that depalmitoylates PRDX5 to regulate mitochondrial antioxidant capacity; required for normal spermatogenesis in mice.

molecular_action: actin-binding scaffold; SRF transcriptional coactivation (via ABRA); cilia biogenesis (actin regulation)
pathways: actin dynamics, SRF/MRTF signaling, ciliogenesis, PKA/cAMP pathway
neural_phenotype: hippocampal engram cell reactivation reduced (contextual fear memory generalization); implicated in dentate gyrus granule cell function
one_line_summary: ABLIM3 is an actin-binding LIM-domain scaffold that stimulates ABRA-SRF transcriptional activity; expressed in hippocampal dentate granule cells where it may regulate memory encoding and neuronal morphology.

molecular_action: telomere protection; recruits POT1 to single-stranded telomeric DNA; enhances telomerase processivity
pathways: telomere maintenance, DNA damage response, shelterin complex
ko_phenotype: adrenocortical dysplasia, hypoplastic gonads, infertility, premature aging in hypomorphic mice
cancer_role: germline mutations predispose to melanoma; telomere shortening drives lung fibrosis
neuro_disease: telomere syndrome (familial ILD, dyskeratosis congenita spectrum), melanoma predisposition
one_line_summary: ACD/TPP1 is a core shelterin complex component that recruits POT1 to single-stranded telomeric DNA and enhances telomerase processivity; germline mutations cause telomere syndromes with cancer predisposition.

key_substrates: long-chain fatty acids (myristate, laurate, arachidonate); phosphatidylcholine biosynthesis substrates
subcellular: ER membrane, lipid droplets, mitochondria-associated membranes
pathways: fatty acid beta-oxidation, lipogenesis, ferroptosis suppression, VLDL assembly
cancer_types: glioblastoma, hepatocellular carcinoma, breast cancer, multiple myeloma, cholangiocarcinoma, prostate cancer
cancer_role: oncogenic (promotes lipid reprogramming, ferroptosis resistance, tumor growth; synthetic lethal with NF2 loss)
one_line_summary: ACSL3 activates long-chain fatty acids to acyl-CoA thioesters, fueling anabolic lipogenesis and suppressing ferroptosis; it is broadly oncogenic across multiple cancer types and synthetic lethal with NF2 loss in Schwann cells.
